 {"id":"bd-1i8n","title":"Idea: Env var allowlist passthrough for remote exec","description":"## Background
Remote builds can diverge from local when environment variables (RUSTFLAGS, CARGO_TARGET_DIR, feature toggles) are not preserved. Today only implicit env is used on workers, which can cause subtle mismatches.

## Goals
- Provide an explicit allowlist of env vars to forward to workers.
- Safe-by-default: nothing sensitive forwarded unless allowed.
- Support global config and project override.

## Design / Approach
- Add config section: `environment.allowlist = ["RUSTFLAGS", "CARGO_TARGET_DIR", ...]`.
- Hook builds an env prefix for remote commands using current process env, filtered by allowlist.
- Include in rsync/SSH command wrapper; ensure quoting is safe.
- Provide `RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST` env override for quick usage.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Extend config schema + validation for allowlist entries.
- Add helper to extract allowed env vars and format for `sh -c` safely.
- Integrate into transfer pipeline (before wrap_command_with_toolchain/color).
- Update docs and `rch config show` output to surface allowlist.

## Tests
- Unit: allowlist parsing and filtering.
- Unit: safe quoting for env values with spaces/quotes.
- Integration: remote command sees forwarded env (mock worker).
- E2E: project config override works and is isolated per project.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Env passthrough is explicit and safe.
- No env leakage unless configured.
- Remote build parity improves for env-dependent builds.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-1i8n.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- Env values with spaces/quotes/newlines must be safely quoted or rejected.
- Missing env vars in allowlist are ignored silently (no error noise).
- Logs must not print raw values for sensitive keys (redact).

## E2E Outline
- Allowlist + env set -> remote command sees value.
- Non-allowlisted env var not present remotely.
- Unsafe env value (newline) is blocked with warning.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs or new helper: env allowlist parsing + safe quoting.
- rch/src/config.rs: allowlist config merge/override.
- rch-common/src/ssh.rs: env prefix formatting for remote command.

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-1i8n.sh: set env vars and verify remote sees allowlisted only.
- Ensure logs redact sensitive values (show key names only).

 {"id":"bd-3c8d","title":"E2E Test Script: Self-Healing Validation","description":"# E2E Test Script: Self-Healing Validation

## Overview
A comprehensive end-to-end test script that validates the entire self-healing system works correctly in real-world scenarios. This script should be runnable by developers and CI to ensure the self-healing features work as designed.

## Test Script Location
`tests/e2e/test_self_healing.sh`

## Script Structure

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# =============================================================================
# E2E Test: RCH Self-Healing System
# =============================================================================
#
# This script validates the mutually reinforcing self-healing behavior:
# 1. Hook auto-starts daemon when unavailable
# 2. Daemon auto-installs hooks on startup
# 3. Doctor --fix repairs both
#
# Prerequisites:
# - rch and rchd binaries in PATH
# - Write access to ~/.claude/ and /tmp/
# - No production daemon running (test uses isolated config)
#
# Usage:
#   ./tests/e2e/test_self_healing.sh [--verbose]
#

# =============================================================================
# Configuration
# =============================================================================

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
TEST_HOME=$(mktemp -d)
TEST_CONFIG_DIR="${TEST_HOME}/.config/rch"
TEST_CLAUDE_DIR="${TEST_HOME}/.claude"
TEST_SOCKET="/tmp/rch-test-$$.sock"
LOG_FILE="${TEST_HOME}/test.log"

VERBOSE="${1:-}"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0
TOTAL=0

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# =============================================================================
# Logging Functions
# =============================================================================

log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N')
    echo -e "[$timestamp] [$level] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_info()  { log "INFO " "$*"; }
log_pass()  { log "${GREEN}PASS${NC} " "$*"; }
log_fail()  { log "${RED}FAIL${NC} " "$*"; }
log_test()  { log "${BLUE}TEST${NC} " "$*"; }
log_detail() { [[ "$VERBOSE" == "--verbose" ]] && log "DEBUG" "$*" || true; }

# =============================================================================
# Test Utilities
# =============================================================================

setup_test_env() {
    log_info "Setting up isolated test environment..."
    log_detail "TEST_HOME=$TEST_HOME"
    
    mkdir -p "$TEST_CONFIG_DIR"
    mkdir -p "$TEST_CLAUDE_DIR"
    
    # Create minimal config pointing to test socket
    cat > "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml" <<EOF
socket_path = "${TEST_SOCKET}"

[self_healing]
hook_starts_daemon = true
daemon_installs_hooks = true
daemon_start_timeout = 5
auto_start_cooldown = 5
EOF
    
    export HOME="$TEST_HOME"
    export RCH_CONFIG="${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml"
    export RCH_SOCKET_PATH="$TEST_SOCKET"
    
    log_info "Test environment ready"
}

cleanup() {
    log_info "Cleaning up test environment..."
    
    # Stop any test daemon
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_detail "Stopping test daemon..."
        rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
    
    # Kill any stray rchd processes from this test
    pkill -f "rchd.*${TEST_SOCKET}" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    # Remove test directory
    if [[ -d "$TEST_HOME" && "$TEST_HOME" == /tmp/* ]]; then
        rm -rf "$TEST_HOME"
    fi
    
    log_info "Cleanup complete"
}

trap cleanup EXIT

assert_eq() {
    local expected=$1
    local actual=$2
    local msg=$3
    
    if [[ "$expected" == "$actual" ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "Assertion failed: $msg"
        log_detail "  Expected: $expected"
        log_detail "  Actual:   $actual"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_file_exists() {
    local file=$1
    local msg=$2
    
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "File not found: $file ($msg)"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_socket_exists() {
    local socket=$1
    local msg=$2
    
    if [[ -S "$socket" ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "Socket not found: $socket ($msg)"
        return 1
    fi
}

assert_contains() {
    local haystack=$1
    local needle=$2
    local msg=$3
    
    if [[ "$haystack" == *"$needle"* ]]; then
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "String not found: '$needle' ($msg)"
        log_detail "  In: $haystack"
        return 1
    fi
}

run_test() {
    local name=$1
    shift
    local test_fn=$1
    
    TOTAL=$((TOTAL + 1))
    log_test "Running: $name"
    
    if $test_fn; then
        PASSED=$((PASSED + 1))
        log_pass "$name"
        return 0
    else
        FAILED=$((FAILED + 1))
        log_fail "$name"
        return 1
    fi
}

# =============================================================================
# Test Cases
# =============================================================================

test_hook_auto_starts_daemon() {
    log_detail "Ensuring daemon is stopped..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    # Verify daemon not running
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Precondition failed: daemon still running"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Creating test project..."
    local test_project="${TEST_HOME}/test_project"
    mkdir -p "$test_project"
    cat > "${test_project}/Cargo.toml" <<EOF
[package]
name = "test_project"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    mkdir -p "${test_project}/src"
    echo 'fn main() { println!("Hello"); }' > "${test_project}/src/main.rs"
    
    log_detail "Invoking hook (should auto-start daemon)..."
    local hook_input='{"event":"PreToolUse","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cargo build --release"},"session_id":"test","session_cwd":"'"$test_project"'"}'
    
    local output
    output=$(echo "$hook_input" | timeout 30 rch 2>&1) || true
    
    log_detail "Hook output: $output"
    
    # Check if daemon was auto-started
    sleep 2
    if ! [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon was not auto-started"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Daemon auto-started successfully"
    return 0
}

test_daemon_auto_installs_hook() {
    log_detail "Removing existing hook..."
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    
    log_detail "Stopping daemon..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Starting daemon (should install hook)..."
    rch daemon start
    sleep 2
    
    # Check if hook was installed
    if ! assert_file_exists "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" "Hook settings file should exist"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Verify hook content
    local settings
    settings=$(cat "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json")
    
    if ! assert_contains "$settings" '"command": "rch"' "Settings should contain rch hook"; then
        log_detail "Settings content: $settings"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Hook auto-installed successfully"
    return 0
}

test_daemon_preserves_existing_hooks() {
    log_detail "Creating settings with existing DCG hook..."
    mkdir -p "$TEST_CLAUDE_DIR"
    cat > "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" <<EOF
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "command": "dcg",
        "description": "Destructive Command Guard"
      }
    ]
  }
}
EOF
    
    log_detail "Restarting daemon..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    rch daemon start
    sleep 2
    
    # Verify both hooks present
    local settings
    settings=$(cat "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json")
    
    if ! assert_contains "$settings" '"command": "dcg"' "DCG hook should be preserved"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! assert_contains "$settings" '"command": "rch"' "RCH hook should be added"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Existing hooks preserved successfully"
    return 0
}

test_doctor_fix_installs_hook() {
    log_detail "Removing hook..."
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    
    log_detail "Running doctor --fix..."
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix 2>&1)
    
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    
    if ! assert_file_exists "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" "Doctor should install hook"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! assert_contains "$output" "Fixed" "Doctor should report fix"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Doctor --fix installed hook successfully"
    return 0
}

test_doctor_fix_starts_daemon() {
    log_detail "Stopping daemon..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Running doctor --fix..."
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix 2>&1)
    
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    
    sleep 2
    if ! assert_socket_exists "$TEST_SOCKET" "Doctor should start daemon"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Doctor --fix started daemon successfully"
    return 0
}

test_doctor_dry_run_no_changes() {
    log_detail "Stopping daemon and removing hook..."
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Running doctor --fix --dry-run..."
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix --dry-run 2>&1)
    
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    
    # Verify no changes made
    if [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        log_fail "Dry run should not create settings file"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Dry run should not start daemon"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if ! assert_contains "$output" "Would" "Dry run should say 'Would'"; then
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_detail "Dry run made no changes as expected"
    return 0
}

test_auto_start_cooldown() {
    log_detail "This test validates cooldown prevents restart spam"
    
    # Start and immediately stop daemon
    rch daemon start
    sleep 1
    rch daemon stop
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    
    # Record time
    local start_time=$(date +%s)
    
    # Try to trigger auto-start via hook
    local test_project="${TEST_HOME}/test_project2"
    mkdir -p "$test_project/src"
    cat > "${test_project}/Cargo.toml" <<EOF
[package]
name = "test_project2"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    echo 'fn main() {}' > "${test_project}/src/main.rs"
    
    local hook_input='{"event":"PreToolUse","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cargo build"},"session_id":"test","session_cwd":"'"$test_project"'"}'
    
    log_detail "First hook invocation..."
    echo "$hook_input" | timeout 10 rch 2>&1 || true
    
    # Check if cooldown file was created
    local cooldown_file="/tmp/rch-autostart-cooldown"
    if [[ -f "$cooldown_file" ]]; then
        log_detail "Cooldown file created"
    fi
    
    log_detail "Second hook invocation (should skip due to cooldown)..."
    local output
    output=$(echo "$hook_input" | timeout 5 rch 2>&1) || true
    
    # The second invocation should be fast (not waiting for daemon)
    # because cooldown prevents restart attempt
    local end_time=$(date +%s)
    local elapsed=$((end_time - start_time))
    
    log_detail "Elapsed time: ${elapsed}s"
    
    # If cooldown works, second invocation should be quick
    # (This is a heuristic test - timing-based)
    return 0
}

test_config_disables_self_healing() {
    log_detail "Creating config with self-healing disabled..."
    cat > "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml" <<EOF
socket_path = "${TEST_SOCKET}"

[self_healing]
hook_starts_daemon = false
daemon_installs_hooks = false
EOF
    
    # Stop daemon and remove hook
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    sleep 1
    
    log_detail "Starting daemon (should NOT install hook)..."
    rch daemon start
    sleep 2
    
    # Hook should NOT be installed (disabled in config)
    if [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        local settings
        settings=$(cat "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json")
        if [[ "$settings" == *'"command": "rch"'* ]]; then
            log_fail "Hook was installed despite being disabled in config"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    log_detail "Self-healing correctly disabled by config"
    
    # Restore default config
    cat > "${TEST_CONFIG_DIR}/config.toml" <<EOF
socket_path = "${TEST_SOCKET}"

[self_healing]
hook_starts_daemon = true
daemon_installs_hooks = true
EOF
    
    return 0
}

test_full_self_healing_cycle() {
    log_detail "=== Full Self-Healing Cycle Test ==="
    
    # Start from completely clean state
    log_detail "Step 1: Clean state"
    rch daemon stop 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    rm -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json"
    rm -rf "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}"
    sleep 1
    
    # Verify nothing exists
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]] || [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        log_fail "Clean state not achieved"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Clean state verified"
    
    # Run doctor --fix (should fix everything)
    log_detail "Step 2: Run doctor --fix"
    local output
    output=$(rch doctor --fix 2>&1)
    log_detail "Doctor output: $output"
    sleep 2
    
    # Verify hook installed
    if ! [[ -f "${TEST_CLAUDE_DIR}/settings.json" ]]; then
        log_fail "Hook not installed by doctor --fix"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Hook installed"
    
    # Verify daemon running
    if ! [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon not started by doctor --fix"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Daemon running"
    
    # Simulate daemon crash
    log_detail "Step 3: Simulate daemon crash"
    pkill -f "rchd.*${TEST_SOCKET}" 2>/dev/null || true
    rm -f "$TEST_SOCKET"
    sleep 1
    
    if [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon still running after simulated crash"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Daemon crashed (simulated)"
    
    # Trigger build (hook should auto-restart daemon)
    log_detail "Step 4: Trigger build (should auto-restart daemon)"
    local test_project="${TEST_HOME}/test_project_cycle"
    mkdir -p "$test_project/src"
    cat > "${test_project}/Cargo.toml" <<EOF
[package]
name = "cycle_test"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
EOF
    echo 'fn main() { println!("cycle test"); }' > "${test_project}/src/main.rs"
    
    local hook_input='{"event":"PreToolUse","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"cargo build --release"},"session_id":"cycle-test","session_cwd":"'"$test_project"'"}'
    
    output=$(echo "$hook_input" | timeout 30 rch 2>&1) || true
    log_detail "Hook output: $output"
    sleep 2
    
    # Verify daemon auto-restarted
    if ! [[ -S "$TEST_SOCKET" ]]; then
        log_fail "Daemon not auto-restarted by hook"
        return 1
    fi
    log_detail "  ✓ Daemon auto-restarted"
    
    log_detail "=== Full cycle complete ==="
    return 0
}

main() {
    echo ""

    echo "=============================================="
    echo ""
    
    setup_test_env
    
    # Run all tests
    run_test "Hook auto-starts daemon" test_hook_auto_starts_daemon
    run_test "Daemon auto-installs hook" test_daemon_auto_installs_hook
    run_test "Daemon preserves existing hooks" test_daemon_preserves_existing_hooks
    run_test "Doctor --fix installs hook" test_doctor_fix_installs_hook
    run_test "Doctor --fix starts daemon" test_doctor_fix_starts_daemon
    run_test "Doctor --dry-run makes no changes" test_doctor_dry_run_no_changes
    run_test "Auto-start cooldown works" test_auto_start_cooldown
    run_test "Config disables self-healing" test_config_disables_self_healing
    run_test "Full self-healing cycle" test_full_self_healing_cycle
    
    # Summary
    echo ""
    echo "=============================================="
    echo "  Test Results"

    echo ""

        exit 0

}

## Executive Summary

This epic encompasses the complete integration of the rich_rust library into RCH (Remote Compilation Helper) to provide beautiful, premium terminal output for human operators watching AI coding agents work. The integration must maintain **zero interference** with AI agents, which are the primary users of RCH.

## Background & Motivation

### The Problem
RCH currently outputs plain text for status, errors, and progress. While functional, this provides a poor experience for humans monitoring AI agents. When operators run 15+ agents simultaneously, they need at-a-glance visual feedback about:
- Which workers are healthy/degraded/offline
- What compilations are in progress
- Where errors occurred and how to fix them
- Transfer and benchmark progress

### The Solution
Integrate rich_rust (a Rust port of Python's Rich library) to provide:
- Styled text with markup syntax ([bold red]text[/])
- Tables with auto-sizing columns and Unicode borders
- Panels for framed content (errors, status)
- Progress bars for transfers and benchmarks
- Color-coded status indicators
- Automatic terminal capability detection and color downgrade

## The Cardinal Rule: Agent-First Architecture

**CRITICAL**: AI coding agents are the PRIMARY users of RCH. They interact through:
1. JSON hook protocol (stdin -> stdout)
2. Exit codes (0, 1, 101, 128+N)
3. Streamed compilation output

Rich formatting must NEVER interfere with these machine interfaces. The design principle is:
- **stderr for humans**: Rich output goes to stderr
- **stdout for machines**: Structured data stays pristine on stdout
- **Graceful degradation**: If rich_rust fails, fall back to plain text

## Output Classification

### NEVER Add Rich Formatting (Machine Output)
- Hook JSON responses (stdout)
- --json flag output (stdout)
- Compilation output passthrough (stdout)
- Test output streaming (stdout)
- Exit codes (process exit status)

### ADD Rich Formatting (Human Output)
- rch status commands (stderr)
- rch workers list/probe/benchmark (stderr)
- rchd startup banner and health (stderr)
- Error messages and warnings (stderr)
- Transfer and benchmark progress (stderr)

## Implementation Phases

1. **Phase 1: Foundation** - Infrastructure, modules, context detection
2. **Phase 2: CLI Commands** - Rich output for status/worker commands
3. **Phase 3: Daemon UI** - Startup banner, health displays
4. **Phase 4: Error Experience** - Beautiful error panels
5. **Phase 5: Progress & Feedback** - Progress bars, spinners
6. **Phase 6: Polish** - Theme refinement, docs, validation

## Success Criteria

1. All rch CLI commands have beautiful, informative output
2. AI agents function identically with rich output enabled/disabled
3. NO_COLOR and FORCE_COLOR environment variables are respected
4. Performance: Zero overhead on hook classification hot path
5. All existing tests pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:06.187551979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T21:00:06.187551979Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3eaa","title":"Worker Preflight Health Guards (Load + Disk)","description":"# Worker Preflight Health Guards (Load Average + Disk Space)

## Merged Scope
This bead consolidates two related worker preflight check features:
- **Load Average Guard** (formerly bd-2hbf): Skip workers with high CPU load
- **Disk Space Guard** (formerly bd-1mij): Skip workers with low free disk

Both are preflight checks that improve worker selection quality by filtering out unhealthy workers before job assignment.

## Background
Even if slots are available, a worker may be unsuitable:
- High load average spikes hurt build times
- Low disk space can cause builds to fail or slow dramatically

Preflight guards prevent wasted transfers and improve overall compilation success rates.

## Goals
1. Skip workers whose load average exceeds a threshold (per-core normalized)
2. Skip workers whose free disk space is below minimum
3. Provide clear selection reasons (NoWorkersWithCapacity, NoWorkersWithDisk)
4. Fail-open: if all workers exceed thresholds, pick best available with warning

## Design / Approach

### Configuration
```toml
[selection]
max_load_per_core = 2.0    # Skip if load1 / cores > threshold
min_free_gb = 10.0         # Skip if free disk < threshold
```

### Telemetry Fields
Extend worker capabilities/telemetry:
- `load_avg_1`, `load_avg_5`, `load_avg_15` (from /proc/loadavg or uptime)
- `disk_free_gb`, `disk_total_gb` (from df or statfs)

### Selection Logic
1. Filter by slot availability
2. Filter by load-per-core threshold
3. Filter by disk space threshold
4. Sort by SpeedScore
5. If all filtered out, pick best available with warning

## Tasks / Subtasks
1. Extend telemetry payload to include load avg and disk metrics
2. Add fallback SSH probe (uptime, df) when telemetry unavailable
3. Add config fields with validation
4. Update selection logic with reason codes
5. Expose in `rch status` and `rch workers list` output
6. Add warnings when using degraded workers

## Tests

### Unit Tests
- `rchd/src/selection.rs`: load-per-core threshold logic, disk space filter
- `rchd/src/telemetry.rs`: load avg parsing, disk metric extraction
- Selection reason codes for each guard type

### Integration Tests
- Mock worker reports high load -> skipped
- Mock worker reports low disk -> skipped
- All workers degraded -> pick best with warning

### E2E Tests
Script: `scripts/e2e_preflight_guards.sh`

Scenarios:
- Mixed load -> skip high, select low
- Mixed disk -> skip low-disk, select adequate
- All high load -> select least-loaded with warning
- All low disk -> select most-available with warning
- Status output displays load and disk warnings

## Acceptance Criteria
- High-load workers are skipped automatically
- Low-disk workers are skipped automatically
- Selection reasons are visible and actionable
- Thresholds are configurable and documented
- Fail-open behavior preserves system usability

## Logging & E2E
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, duration_ms, result, error
- Phases: classify, select (with guard reasons), sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary

## Edge Cases & UX
- Missing telemetry -> allow worker but warn
- Use load-per-core to normalize across heterogeneous machines
- Support per-worker threshold overrides
- Selection reason includes specific guard signals

 {"id":"bd-dej3","title":"Feature Flag Verification Test (rich-ui compile-time toggle)","description":"## Test: rich-ui Feature Flag Toggle

Verify that the rich-ui feature can be completely disabled at compile time without breaking any functionality.

### Why This Matters

1. Users may want minimal dependencies
2. Embedded/constrained environments may not support terminals
3. Provides fallback if rich_rust causes issues
4. Ensures clean separation between core functionality and UI

### Test Implementation

```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/test_feature_flag.sh
set -euo pipefail

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*"; }
pass() { log "✓ PASS: $*"; }
fail() { log "✗ FAIL: $*"; exit 1; }

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 1: Build WITHOUT rich-ui feature
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 1: Build without rich-ui feature"

cargo build -p rch --no-default-features --release --target-dir target/no-rich 2>&1 || {
    fail "Failed to build without rich-ui feature"
}

NO_RICH="./target/no-rich/release/rch"
if [[ \! -x "$NO_RICH" ]]; then
    fail "Binary not created"
fi
pass "Build without rich-ui succeeded"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 2: Build WITH rich-ui feature  
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 2: Build with rich-ui feature"

cargo build -p rch --features rich-ui --release --target-dir target/with-rich 2>&1 || {
    fail "Failed to build with rich-ui feature"
}

WITH_RICH="./target/with-rich/release/rch"
pass "Build with rich-ui succeeded"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 3: Binary size comparison
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 3: Binary size comparison"

SIZE_NO_RICH=$(stat -f%z "$NO_RICH" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$NO_RICH")
SIZE_WITH_RICH=$(stat -f%z "$WITH_RICH" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$WITH_RICH")

DIFF=$((SIZE_WITH_RICH - SIZE_NO_RICH))
PERCENT=$(echo "scale=1; $DIFF * 100 / $SIZE_NO_RICH" | bc)

log "Binary sizes: no-rich=${SIZE_NO_RICH} with-rich=${SIZE_WITH_RICH} diff=+${DIFF} (+${PERCENT}%)"

# Warn if rich-ui adds more than 50% size
if (( DIFF > SIZE_NO_RICH / 2 )); then
    log "WARNING: rich-ui adds significant binary size (+${PERCENT}%)"
fi
pass "Binary size measured"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 4: Core functionality works WITHOUT rich-ui
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 4: Core functionality without rich-ui"

# Hook classification must work
echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | "$NO_RICH" hook --stdin > /tmp/hook_no_rich.json 2>&1 || {
    fail "Hook failed without rich-ui"
}

if \! jq -e . /tmp/hook_no_rich.json >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    fail "Hook output not valid JSON without rich-ui"
fi
pass "Hook works without rich-ui"

# Status command must work
"$NO_RICH" status > /tmp/status_no_rich.txt 2>&1 || true
pass "Status command runs without rich-ui"

# Workers list must work
"$NO_RICH" workers list > /tmp/workers_no_rich.txt 2>&1 || true
pass "Workers list runs without rich-ui"

# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TEST 5: Outputs are functionally equivalent
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
log "TEST 5: Functional equivalence"

# Hook output should be identical
echo '{"tool":"Bash","input":{"command":"echo test"}}' | "$WITH_RICH" hook --stdin > /tmp/hook_with_rich.json 2>&1

if \! diff /tmp/hook_no_rich.json /tmp/hook_with_rich.json; then
    fail "Hook output differs between builds\!"
fi
pass "Hook output identical"

# --json output should be identical
"$NO_RICH" status --json > /tmp/status_no_rich.json 2>&1 || true
"$WITH_RICH" status --json > /tmp/status_with_rich.json 2>&1 || true

if [[ -s /tmp/status_no_rich.json ]] && [[ -s /tmp/status_with_rich.json ]]; then
    if \! diff /tmp/status_no_rich.json /tmp/status_with_rich.json; then
        fail "--json output differs between builds\!"
    fi
    pass "--json output identical"
else
    log "SKIP: Status --json not available (daemon not running)"
fi

log ""
log "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"
log "ALL FEATURE FLAG TESTS PASSED"
log "═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════"

 {"id":"bd-g6vh","title":"Idea: Transfer size estimator + skip heuristics","description":"## Background
Some projects are too large to benefit from remote execution due to transfer overhead. A size estimator prevents wasting time and bandwidth.

## Goals
- Estimate upload/download size and expected transfer time before offloading.
- Skip remote execution if estimated transfer exceeds a configurable threshold.
- Provide transparent reasoning in verbose logs.

## Design / Approach
- Use rsync dry-run (`--stats --dry-run`) to estimate bytes/files for upload.
- Combine with configured bandwidth estimate or measured link latency to derive transfer time.
- Add config: max_transfer_mb, max_transfer_time_ms.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Implement dry-run estimator with mock support.
- Add config settings and documentation.
- Integrate gating into hook (after classification, before worker selection).

## Tests
- Unit: parse rsync --stats output.
- Integration: estimator returns expected values for fixtures.
- E2E: remote skipped when transfer exceeds threshold.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Remote offload is skipped for excessively large transfers.
- Estimator adds minimal latency (<5ms average for small projects).

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-g6vh.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- rsync dry-run unavailable: skip estimator and proceed (fail-open).
- Cache estimator result briefly to avoid repeated overhead.
- Include .rchignore + config excludes in estimate.

## E2E Outline
- Small fixture below threshold -> remote allowed.
- Large fixture above threshold -> local allowed.
- Verbose output includes estimated bytes + time.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: parse rsync --stats output and bytes calculation.
- rch/src/hook.rs: estimator failure falls back to normal flow (fail-open).

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-g6vh.sh: small vs large fixture; verify skip threshold behavior.
- Logs include estimated bytes + transfer time.

 {"id":"bd-ix44","title":"Create UI module structure in rch-common, rch, and rchd","description":"# Create UI module structure in rch-common, rch, and rchd

## Task Description

Create the mod.rs files and directory structure for UI modules across all three crates. This establishes the module hierarchy without implementing functionality.

## Background

We need a consistent module structure:
- rch-common: Shared types (context, theme, icons)
- rch: CLI-specific components (console, tables, progress)
- rchd: Daemon-specific components (banner, dashboard)

## Implementation

### rch-common/src/ui/mod.rs

```rust
//! Shared UI utilities for RCH
//!
//! This module provides the foundational types and utilities used by
//! both the rch CLI and rchd daemon for rich terminal output.
//!
//! # Module Organization
//!
//! - `context`: Output context detection (interactive, machine, etc.)
//! - `theme`: Color constants and style generators
//! - `icons`: Unicode icons with ASCII fallbacks
//!
//! # Design Philosophy
//!
//! The UI system is designed with agent-first principles:
//! - Rich output goes to stderr, machine data to stdout
//! - Context detection prevents rich output in hook mode
//! - All functions gracefully degrade to plain text

#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
mod context;
#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
mod theme;
#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
mod icons;

#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
pub use context::OutputContext;
#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
pub use theme::RchTheme;
#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
pub use icons::Icons;

// Stub implementations when rich-ui is disabled
#[cfg(not(feature = "rich-ui"))]
mod stubs {
    #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    pub enum OutputContext {
        Plain,
    }

    impl OutputContext {
        pub fn detect() -> Self { Self::Plain }
        pub fn supports_rich(&self) -> bool { false }
        pub fn supports_color(&self) -> bool { false }
        pub fn is_machine(&self) -> bool { false }
    }
}

#[cfg(not(feature = "rich-ui"))]
pub use stubs::OutputContext;
```

### rch/src/ui/mod.rs

```rust
//! Rich terminal output for rch CLI
//!
//! This module provides beautiful, context-aware terminal output
//! for human operators watching RCH in action.
//!
//! # Module Organization
//!
//! - `console`: Context-aware Console wrapper (RchConsole)
//! - `components/`: High-level UI components
//!   - `status_table`: Worker and job status tables
//!   - `error_panel`: Beautiful error display
//!   - `progress`: Transfer and benchmark progress
//! - `formatters/`: Data formatting utilities
//!   - `worker`: Worker state formatting
//!   - `job`: Job information formatting
//!
//! # Usage
//!
//! ```rust
//! use rch::ui::console::CONSOLE;
//!
//! CONSOLE.print_success("Operation completed");
//! CONSOLE.print_error("Failed", "Connection refused");
//! ```

#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
mod console;
#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
pub mod components;
#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
pub mod formatters;

#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
pub use console::{RchConsole, CONSOLE};

// Re-export common types for convenience
#[cfg(feature = "rich-ui")]
pub use rch_common::ui::{OutputContext, RchTheme, Icons};

// Stub when disabled
#[cfg(not(feature = "rich-ui"))]
pub mod console {
    pub struct RchConsole;
    impl RchConsole {
        pub fn new() -> Self { Self }
        pub fn print_plain(&self, s: &str) { eprintln!("{}", s); }
        pub fn print_error(&self, t: &str, m: &str) {
            eprintln!("Error: {}\n{}", t, m);
        }
    }
    pub static CONSOLE: RchConsole = RchConsole;
}
```

 {"id":"bd-j9z9","title":"Create Test Runner Script (scripts/run_true_e2e.sh)","description":"## Purpose
Create a comprehensive test runner script that executes all true e2e tests with proper logging, reporting, and CI integration.

## Why This Matters
- Manual test execution is error-prone
- CI systems need standardized entry point
- Developers need easy local testing
- Reports needed for tracking regressions

## Script: scripts/run_true_e2e.sh

### Basic Usage
```bash
# Run all true e2e tests
./scripts/run_true_e2e.sh

# Run specific test category
./scripts/run_true_e2e.sh --filter cargo

# Run with verbose logging
./scripts/run_true_e2e.sh --verbose

# Skip if no workers (CI mode)
./scripts/run_true_e2e.sh --ci
```

### Script Features

#### 1. Pre-flight Checks
- Verify daemon is running (or start it)
- Verify at least one worker is reachable
- Verify required tools (rsync, ssh, etc.)
- Check Rust toolchain versions match

#### 2. Test Execution
- Run tests with cargo test --features true-e2e
- Capture all output (stdout, stderr, timing)
- Handle test timeouts gracefully
- Support parallel test execution

#### 3. Logging Integration
- Set RCH_E2E_LOG_DIR to timestamped directory
- Collect all test logs
- Generate summary log

#### 4. Report Generation
- JUnit XML for CI systems
- HTML report with timing graphs
- Summary table (pass/fail/skip counts)
- Failure details with log excerpts

### Output Structure
```
test-results/
├── run_2024-01-19_120000/
│   ├── junit.xml           # CI-compatible report
│   ├── report.html         # Human-readable report
│   ├── summary.json        # Machine-readable summary
│   ├── logs/
│   │   ├── test_cargo_build.jsonl
│   │   ├── test_cargo_test.jsonl
│   │   └── ...
│   └── artifacts/
│       └── (any test artifacts)
```

### CI Integration
```yaml
# GitHub Actions example
- name: Run True E2E Tests
  run: ./scripts/run_true_e2e.sh --ci --junit test-results/junit.xml
  env:
    RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG: ${{ secrets.WORKERS_CONFIG }}
    
- name: Upload Test Results
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: e2e-test-results
    path: test-results/
```

### Exit Codes
- 0: All tests passed
- 1: Some tests failed
- 2: Infrastructure error (no workers, daemon failed)
- 3: Configuration error
